{"society_power": 7.0, "state_power": 7.9, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.1, "key_event": "Indira Gandhi's personalization of power amid rising mobilization", "reasoning": "The state's despotic reach grew (bank nationalization, License Raj, 1971 war victory, nuclear test, personalized Congress dominance), but this was partly offset by economic crises, food-aid dependence, and institutional erosion, leaving state power roughly stable-to-slightly-up. Society power holds steady as new checks (Kesavananda, end of one-party dominance, JP Movement, labor mobilization) balanced the weakening of intermediary institutions and centralized executive dominance."}
